description_long: This temple features a large, open-air stepped seating area reminiscent of an ancient Roman or Greek theater, with rows of stone benches radiating out from a central stage or altar. The structure is partially covered by a terracotta-tiled gable roof supported by columns, providing shelter to the rear section and possibly the main sanctum. The uncovered seating area is expansive, with a clear division between the roofed and open sections, and a rectangular green area at the center, likely representing a ceremonial or performance space. The building's materials appear to be primarily stone for the seating and structural elements, with clay tiles for the roof. The overall form is rectangular, with the roofed section occupying one end and the stepped seating extending outward. The design is austere, with minimal visible ornamentation and a focus on geometric lines and functional layout.
description: A rectangular stone temple with a large stepped seating area and a terracotta-tiled gable roof covering the rear section. The structure is open-air with minimal ornamentation and a central green ceremonial space.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stepped stone seating, the gable roof covering the rear, and the open central area.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full extent of the stepped seating, the rectangular green area, and the roofed section at the back.
description_view_2: Opposite side view focusing on the length of the seating rows and the full span of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Rear view dominated by the terracotta-tiled roof, with little of the seating visible.
attribute_ornamentation: minimalist, geometric lines, no visible decorative motifs
attribute_entrance_design: Wide open stepped approach with no distinct framed entrance, leading directly into the seating and central area
